tool.name: Trackastra
tool.description: Track cell instances in time-lapse OME-Zarr datasets
tool.command: python '$__tool_directory__/trackastra_wrapper.py' $mode_cond.mode --zarr_path '$mode_cond.zarr_path' --scale_level $mode_cond.scale_level --downscale_x $mode_cond.downscale_x --downscale_y $mode_cond.downscale_y --downscale_z $mode_cond.downscale_z --start_tp $mode_cond.start_tp --end_tp $mode_cond.end_tp #if $mode_cond.mode == 'segment_and_track' --channel_coords '$mode_cond.channel_coords' --segmentation_model $mode_cond.segmentation_model --tracking_model $mode_cond.tracking_model #else --raw_channel_coords '$mode_cond.raw_channel_coords' --seg_channel_coords '$mode_cond.seg_channel_coords' --tracking_model $mode_cond.tracking_model #end if && cp trackastra_trackgraph.pickle.dat '$output_trackgraph' && cp napari_tracks.pickle.dat '$output_napari_tracks'

param.name: mode
param.label: Processing mode
param.help: Choose between segmentation+tracking or tracking only
param.option: Segment cells and track (Cellpose v3 + Trackastra)

param.name: zarr_path
param.label: Zarr dataset path
param.help: URL or local path to OME-Zarr dataset. Can be https://, s3://, or local ./path. Example: https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0101A/13457537.zarr/0

param.name: scale_level
param.label: Pyramid scale level
param.help: Resolution level in zarr pyramid. 0=finest resolution (best quality but slower), higher numbers=lower resolution but faster processing

param.name: downscale_x
param.label: Downscale factor X
param.help: Spatial downscaling for X dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution). Useful for large images (500+ pixels) to speed up processing

param.name: downscale_y
param.label: Downscale factor Y
param.help: Spatial downscaling for Y dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution)

param.name: downscale_z
param.label: Downscale factor Z
param.help: Spatial downscaling for Z dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution) for 3D tracking

param.name: start_tp
param.label: Start time point
param.help: First frame to process (0-indexed). Use to test on subset of frames

param.name: end_tp
param.label: End time point
param.help: Last frame to process. Use -1 to process all frames

param.name: channel_coords
param.label: Channel coordinate
param.help: For multi-dimensional zarr, specify which dimensions/coordinates select the raw channel. Space or comma-separated. Example: '0' or '0 1' for 6D data

param.name: segmentation_model
param.label: Segmentation model
param.help: Cellpose v3 model to use for cell/nuclei detection

param.name: tracking_model
param.label: Tracking model
param.help: Trackastra model for tracking cells across frames

param.name: zarr_path
param.label: Zarr dataset path
param.help: URL or local path to OME-Zarr dataset. Can be https://, s3://, or local ./path. Example: https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0101A/13457537.zarr/0

param.name: scale_level
param.label: Pyramid scale level
param.help: Resolution level in zarr pyramid. 0=finest resolution (best quality but slower), higher numbers=lower resolution but faster processing

param.name: downscale_x
param.label: Downscale factor X
param.help: Spatial downscaling for X dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution). Useful for large images (500+ pixels) to speed up processing

param.name: downscale_y
param.label: Downscale factor Y
param.help: Spatial downscaling for Y dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution)

param.name: downscale_z
param.label: Downscale factor Z
param.help: Spatial downscaling for Z dimension (1.0=no scaling, 2.0=half resolution) for 3D tracking

param.name: start_tp
param.label: Start time point
param.help: First frame to process (0-indexed). Use to test on subset of frames

param.name: end_tp
param.label: End time point
param.help: Last frame to process. Use -1 to process all frames

param.name: raw_channel_coords
param.label: Raw channel coordinate
param.help: For multi-dimensional zarr, specify coordinates to select the raw image channel. Space or comma-separated

param.name: seg_channel_coords
param.label: Segmentation channel coordinate
param.help: For multi-dimensional zarr, specify coordinates to select the segmentation channel. Space or comma-separated

param.name: tracking_model
param.label: Tracking model
param.help: Trackastra model for tracking cells across frames

tool.help:
**Trackastra: Deep Learning-based Cell Tracking in Zarr Datasets**
This tool performs automated cell instance tracking in time-lapse OME-Zarr (NGFF) datasets. It supports two workflows:
1. **Segment and Track**: Automatically segments cell instances using Cellpose v3, then performs tracking using Trackastra 2. **Track Only**: Performs tracking on pre-segmented data (segmentation provided by other tools)
**Input Format**
The tool requires OME-Zarr (NGFF) formatted multi-dimensional arrays. The expected dimensions are:
- **t**: Time (required) - number of frames/timepoints - **z**: Depth (optional for 2D data) - number of z-slices - **y, x**: Spatial dimensions (required) - image height and width
Additional dimensions (such as channel, view angle, domain) are supported but must be navigated using the coordinate parameters.
**Zarr Dataset Paths**
The zarr_path can be:
- **HTTP/HTTPS URL**: https://uk1s3.embassy.ebi.ac.uk/idr/zarr/v0.4/idr0101A/13457537.zarr/0 - **S3 path**: s3://bucket-name/path/to/data.zarr - **Local path**: ./path/to/local/data.zarr (on Galaxy server)
**Key Parameters**
- **scale_level**: Select pyramid resolution level. 0 is the finest resolution. Use higher levels for faster processing with lower resolution. - **Downscale factors (x, y, z)**: Apply additional spatial downscaling. Values >1.0 reduce resolution (e.g., 2.0 = half resolution). Useful for large images to speed up processing. - **Time point range**: Use start_tp and end_tp to process a subset of frames (useful for testing). - **Channel coordinates**: For multi-dimensional datasets, specify which dimensions index the raw data channel. For example, a t,c,z,y,x dataset needs coordinate '0' to select channel 0.
**Segmentation Models (Cellpose v3)**
- **cyto3**: Recommended for general cell types. Best for most cytoplasmic structures. - **cyto2**: Older Cellpose model. Available for compatibility. - **nuclei**: Specialized model for nuclear segmentation.
**Outputs**
The tool generates two pickle files:
1. **trackastra_trackgraph.pickle.dat**: Native Trackastra tracking object containing the complete tracking results in Python pickle format 2. **napari_tracks.pickle.dat**: Formatted tracking results ready for napari visualization
**Using Napari for Visualization**
To inspect tracking results:
1. Open napari in Galaxy 2. In napari's IPython console::
pip install napari-ome-zarr viewer.open("https://path/to/same/zarr/as/above") import pickle with open("napari_tracks.pickle.dat", "rb") as f: tracks = pickle.load(f) viewer.add_tracks(tracks)
3. The tracks will overlay on the original image data for inspection and analysis.
**Advanced: Multi-Dimensional Navigation**
For zarr datasets with extra dimensions beyond t,z,y,x (e.g., t,c,z,y,x where c is channel):
1. **Extra dimensions are pinned to front**: The tool moves non-tzyx dimensions to the front during processing 2. **Coordinates select sub-dimensions**: Provide integer coordinates for each extra dimension
- Example: 6D data (t,view,domain,z,y,x) needs 2 coordinates to pin view and domain - Use "0 1" to select view=0, domain=1
**Performance Tips**
- For large images (500+ pixels/dimension), use downscale factors 2.0+ to reduce computation - Test on a subset with start_tp/end_tp before processing entire datasets - Use lower scale_level (higher resolution) for better segmentation accuracy, but expect slower processing - S3 datasets stream data, so may be slower than local/http access
**References**
- Trackastra: https://github.com/ybnd/trackastra - Cellpose: https://github.com/mouseland/cellpose - OME-Zarr/NGFF: https://ngff.openmicroscopy.org/
